Develop and share best practices across BST community. Foster communication between BU and IT organization. Ensure (with HR support) that the area of responsibility is properly organized, staffed, skilled and directed. Guides, motivates and develops direct and indirect subordinates within HR policies, for the benefit of both company and the employee. Your background Minimum 15+ years of professional working experience in IT. 10+ years' experience working in global and diverse organizations with understanding of Infrastructure, Applications and platforms. A bachelor's degree is required; a master's degree is preferred. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Highly organized with attention to detail. Proven success working in matrixed organizations. Capable of successfully leading remote teams across multiple time zones More about us: Hitachi Energy is a global technology leader that is advancing a sustainable energy future for all. We serve customers in the utility, industry and infrastructure sectors with innovative solutions and services across the value chain. Together with customers and partners, we pioneer technologies and enable the digital transformation required to accelerate the energy transition toward
descriptionByLocale.fr: The opportunity The Grid Automation Business Unit of Hitachi Energy is seeking a dynamic Global IT Leader for the role of Global BST Manager, Grid Automation, to lead an existing team of over forty IT Professionals in supporting and advancing Grid Automation's global businesses and digital IT transformation initiatives. Business Solution Teams (BSTs) in each Business Unit (BU) have complete responsibility for managing BU-specific applications and Infrastructure services, including service delivery. They oversee and control these applications and services while adhering to global governance and security standards.  BSTs design and implement the IT Roadmap and Application Landscape for their respective BUs to align with business requirements. They closely collaborate with global departments to help deliver function-wide applications and enterprise platforms. How you will make an impact Lead Business relationship management with BUs including BST Leadership and guidance across BU\/HUB\/GPG\/Functions. Lead IT Demand Mgt to meet Business requirements. Management and governance of the BST IT organization to support and deliver on Demand priorities. Accountable for Application Management for BU specific applications, Infrastructure\/ Onsite Service Delivery Operational Management of BST assigned Sites. Support IT\/OT Cybersecurity Onsite Service Delivery Operational Management of BST assigned Sites. Digital Transformation IT Build Deployments to support growth of business. Accountable for the total IT offering to the business and provide interface to relevant IT partners, articulating the needs of the business to the relevant IT partners to ensure impactful support to the business. Ensure alignment of IT roadmap to business needs through communication and active feedback loops between stakeholders and IT organization. Define and implement strategic IT priorities in the organization in line with BU strategy and Strategic Business initiatives, covering business functions application landscape, utilizing the existing IT solutions portfolio or ensuring to provide new solutions, where appropriate. Understand the business demands, the current and long-term business strategy and translate them into opportunities for IT to add value to the business. Implement and ensure compliance with global and local standards, tools, processes related to operations\/projects execution. Analyze results of internal and external audits and identify common areas of improvement within the organization. Provide the BU escalation point for all IT related issues. jobs.title: Global BU Sales Leader - Grid Automation
jobs.description: Join Hitachi Energy's Grid Automation business as our Global Head of Sales. You will lead a global team driving commercial strategy and sales execution across a portfolio that includes enterprise software, automation and communications, power conversion solutions and services. This is a high-impact leadership role at the center of our global go-to-market transformation (Suisen)—focused on customer connection, agility, empowerment, and commercial excellence. You will work across product lines and regions to align diverse sales teams and deliver growth, while shaping how we serve customers in a rapidly evolving energy landscape. The location of this role is flexible globally and will be determined in subsequent stages. How You'll Make an Impact Lead global sales performance across all Grid Automation offerings. Manage a small central team of industry leaders and functional experts focused on global sales strategy and governance. Provide dotted-line leadership to sales leaders in global product groups and regional hubs. Align multiple sales operating models under a unified go-to-market strategy. Drive forecasting discipline, pipeline health, and deal governance. Champion customer-first sales execution and strategic account leadership. Accelerate growth in software, digital, and lifecycle-based offerings. Expand reach through partner ecosystems and channel programs. Collaborate with regional, product, and functional leaders to ensure execution. Lead through transformation, simplifying structures and empowering teams. Your Background 15+ years in global sales leadership roles in energy, software, or industrial sectors. Proven success managing complex, matrixed sales organizations. Experience leading sales transformation or go-to-market redesigns. Strong commercial acumen and forecasting discipline. Familiarity with enterprise software, automation, and power systems. Executive presence and ability to influence across functions and geographies. Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred. Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus.

jobs.title: Technical Buyer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy, High Voltage Products, is renowned worldwide as a specialist for power transmission and distribution. The Service unit is responsible for the worldwide after sales support of generator circuit-breaker (GCB) systems and gas insulated switchgears (GIS), including training, installation and commissioning, scheduled maintenance, added value services, troubleshooting, extensions, retrofits and replacements. As a Technical Buyer, you act as a key interface between Procurement, Engineering, Quality, and our global supplier network. You ensure that technically specified materials, components, and services are clearly defined, technically clarified, qualified, and available in line with project, quality, and delivery requirements. Through close cross‑functional collaboration, you translate engineering requirements into robust sourcing solutions and support supplier qualification and technical alignment. Your role contributes directly to product quality, supply reliability, and operational excellence, enabling Hitachi Energy to deliver innovative and sustainable energy solutions to our customers worldwide. How you'll make an impact Perform technical clarification of materials, drawings, specifications, and engineering changes in close collaboration with Engineering and suppliers Support and coordinate Production Part Approval Process (PPAP) activities, first samples, and supplier qualification processes Conduct technical evaluations of alternative materials, deviation requests, and obsolescence cases Collaborate closely with Strategic and Operational Buyers on supplier- and material-related topics Support the handling of supplier complaints, Non‑Conformity Reports (NCRs), and root cause analyses Support and participate in supplier assessments and technical supplier evaluations, including capability, quality, and process maturity reviews Contribute to supplier performance evaluations together with Quality and Strategic Procurement Ensure accuracy and completeness of material and supplier master data in SAP and act as the technical contact point during supplier escalations Support Service Organization and Project Teams in complex or urgent procurement topics Your background Completed technical education (e.g. Polymechanic, Automation Technician, Mechanical Technician, Design Engineer or similar) 3-5 years experience in technical procurement or SCM‑related roles Strong understanding of technical drawings, Bill of Material and manufacturing processes Experience working with suppliers on technical and quality‑related topics Structured, solution‑oriented, and communicative working style Good German skills and very good English skills SAP knowledge Proactive, structured, and solution-focused working style. Team oriented mindset with strong cross-functional collaboration More about us   Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Mitarbeiter/in Endprüfung 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Als Mitarbeiter Endprüfung bei Hitachi Energy bist du ein entscheidendes Bindeglied zwischen Produktion und Auslieferung. Du stellst sicher, dass unsere Produkte vor der Freigabe höchsten Qualitäts-, Sicherheits- und Dokumentationsanforderungen entsprechen. Mit deinem Qualitätsbewusstsein und deinem strukturierten Vorgehen trägst du maßgeblich zur Zuverlässigkeit unserer Produkte und zur Zufriedenheit unserer Kunden bei. Das kannst du bewirken Testen von Generatorleistungsschaltern im Prüffeld (80%): Prüfen der Generator Leistungsschalter gemäss vorgegebenem Prüfplan Bewertung der Messergebnisse Durchführung von Kundenabnahmen in Englischer Sprache Erkennen von mechanischen und elektrischen Fehlern Analyse und Korrektur von Verdrahtungsfehlern im Steuerschrank Übernahme der Verantwortung für das Prüfen der zugeteilten Schalter (zeitlicher Ablauf sowie Qualität der Prüfung) Mitarbeit bei der Verbesserung des Messequipments sowie der Prüfprozesse Montage- und Verdrahtungsarbeiten (20%): Selbstständige Verdrahtung von Baugruppen und Schaltersystemen gemäss Arbeitspapieren wie Kundenauftrag, Elektroschema, Checkliste und Arbeits­plan Montage von PGHV-Produkten entsprechend der in den Qualitätssicherungsverfahren und Arbeitsanweisungen festgehaltenen Vorgaben und Zielsetzungen Lesen und verstehen von technischen Zeichnungen und Stücklisten Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene elektrotechnische Grundausbildung (Automatiker EFZ, o.ä.) Erfahrung im lesen und verstehen von elektrischen Schemas, Montagezeichnungen und Arbeitsanweisungen Kenntnisse der Prüfung elektrischer Maschinen und von Steuerschränken sind von Vorteil Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (mündlich, schriftlich) Affinität zu Computern (Messequipment, diverse Softwaretools, Dokumentation erstellen und pflegen) Gutes Verständnis für Arbeitssicherheit, hohes Qualitätsbewusstsein sowie kundenorientiertes Denken und professioneller Umgang mit Kunden Mehr über uns Hitachi Energy steht für innovative Technologien und nachhaltige Energielösungen weltweit. Bei uns erwartet dich ein sicherer Arbeitsplatz in einem zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen, spannende Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten, ein kollegiales und wertschätzendes Arbeitsumfeld sowie attraktive Sozialleistungen.

descriptionByLocale.de: Über die Position Als Mitarbeiter Endprüfung bei Hitachi Energy bist du ein entscheidendes Bindeglied zwischen Produktion und Auslieferung. Du stellst sicher, dass unsere Produkte vor der Freigabe höchsten Qualitäts-, Sicherheits- und Dokumentationsanforderungen entsprechen. Mit deinem Qualitätsbewusstsein und deinem strukturierten Vorgehen trägst du maßgeblich zur Zuverlässigkeit unserer Produkte und zur Zufriedenheit unserer Kunden bei. Das kannst du bewirken Testen von Generatorleistungsschaltern im Prüffeld (80%): Prüfen der Generator Leistungsschalter gemäss vorgegebenem Prüfplan Bewertung der Messergebnisse Durchführung von Kundenabnahmen in Englischer Sprache Erkennen von mechanischen und elektrischen Fehlern Analyse und Korrektur von Verdrahtungsfehlern im Steuerschrank Übernahme der Verantwortung für das Prüfen der zugeteilten Schalter (zeitlicher Ablauf sowie Qualität der Prüfung) Mitarbeit bei der Verbesserung des Messequipments sowie der Prüfprozesse Montage- und Verdrahtungsarbeiten (20%): Selbstständige Verdrahtung von Baugruppen und Schaltersystemen gemäss Arbeitspapieren wie Kundenauftrag, Elektroschema, Checkliste und Arbeits­plan Montage von PGHV-Produkten entsprechend der in den Qualitätssicherungsverfahren und Arbeitsanweisungen festgehaltenen Vorgaben und Zielsetzungen Lesen und verstehen von technischen Zeichnungen und Stücklisten Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene elektrotechnische Grundausbildung (Automatiker EFZ, o.ä.) jobs.title: Global Product Specialist – Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) are emerging as critical infrastructure for modern data centers, particularly AI facilities, by providing high efficiency (up to 98.5%), compact, and intelligent power management. Unlike conventional transformers, SSTs utilize power electronics to convert medium-voltage grid power directly to 800V DC, significantly reducing footprint, increasing efficiency, and easing grid interconnection. Join us as a Global Product Specialist – Solid-State Transformers (SSTs) and play a pivotal role in shaping the future of data center supply infrastructure, guiding both Hitachi Energy and clients toward smarter, greener, and more resilient operations. If you combine a solid understanding of relevant technologies with insight into customer value drivers—and can translate customer needs into actionable requirements that keep our solutions both innovative and aligned with client expectations—then this role is an excellent fit for you. How you´ll make an impact Conduct market evaluations, competitor analyses, and identify gaps in product, solutions, and services, while assessing Hitachi Energy's positioning Gather and analyze global standards, market requirements, customer needs, and values to contribute to the product roadmap Support the Global Product Manager in creating business cases for product, solutions, and service development projects Support global and local marketing teams in creating market communication materials, including assistance with new product launches Provide sales support, participate in customer meetings, and attend trade shows as required, ensuring the availability of appropriate training materials for internal and external use Drive technical solutions across Hitachi Energy's global footprint, following an annual cycle of activities to govern Global Product Management Your background Bachelor's degree in engineering, along with 10+ years of related experience preferred Prior experience in Product Management or Product Ownership within data centre domain, and knowledge of Solid-State Transformer (SST) technology Possess a technical mindset to serve as a technical expert, instilling customer confidence in the product and contributing to product design Ability to simplify and present the value proposition of a complex offering Strong expertise in qualitative and quantitative business analysis Proficient in analyzing market potential through product segmentation, go-to-market channels, and developing business cases. Willingness to travel domestically and internationally (20%) More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Legal Counsel 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
As Legal Counsel, you will play a key role in our legal team by providing comprehensive legal support to several Business Units and central Functions. As a generalist you cover all fields of contract and commercial law, with an additional focus on public procurement law, R&D, labour law, and data privacy. 
• Your responsibilities will encompass the management and execution of all types of contracts, overseeing potential disputes, and handling various day-to-day business operations. If you possess refined legal skills, can tackle complex issues within an international corporate setting, and are determined, energetic, and capable of influencing at all levels, we invite you to join our team! How you will make an impact: Comprehensive legal support to Hitachi Energy's Business Units and central Functions in all domestic and international legal matters, in particular in the field of contract and commercial law, with an additional focus on public procurement, R&D, Labour Law, and Data Privacy Offer targeted legal advice to support key strategic goals, safeguard the company's reputation and interests, and ensure compliance with relevant laws, regulations and internal policies Communicate and work cross-functionally with other legal counsels, and your stakeholders namely in Sales, SCM, Research, and Management ﻿﻿Provide training to internal clients on relevant legal and contractual topics ﻿﻿Develop and implement standards, policies, and procedures related to your function Advise on the resolution of pre-litigation claims and disputes and handle complex commercial litigation mat-ters, including selecting, engaging, and managing outside counsel, and collaborating with Hitachi Energy local counsels Your background: Master's degree in law, preferably with additional qualifications acquired in the US or UK Admission to the Bar in Switzerland Legal generalist with experience as in-house counsel or attorney-at-law in contract and commercial law. Knowledge in R&D, Labour Law, and Data Privacy is a plus Business- and solution-driven approach with yet the ability to provide accurate, high-quality legal advice and a sound judgment in developing and implementing legal solutions Responsiveness and high degree of responsibility with the willingness to think “out of the box” Ability to work under pressure and in a cross-functional and cross-cultural environment that is changing and may be challenging Well-developed oral and written communication skills in German and English; French and Italian are an advantage More about us : At Hitachi Energy, we believe that diversity powers innovation and collaboration. Our teams are made up of individuals from a wide range of backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, working together across borders and disciplines to shape a more sustainable energy future. We are committed to fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered to contribute their best.

jobs.title: Laboratory Manager – High Voltage Testing 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Our local product group Insulation & Components is the technology leader in design and manufacture of high volt-age transformer bushings. All renowned international companies of the electrical industry, active in power genera-tion, transmission and distribution are among our customers. We have a top position in the high voltage trans-former bushing areas worldwide. In this highly responsible and dynamic role, you will lead our laboratory team at MICAFIL in Zürich-Altstetten (ZH) and ensure that we further strengthen our competitiveness and develop our Swiss branch to meet the global market growth in the field of energy. In this important role, you will continuously drive forward the efficient and innovative processes of our 3 different laboratories together with a strong team. If you have a passion for leadership and high voltage testing, we offer you the stage to bring about real change and actively shape the future of our production processes. How you'll make an impact Lead the laboratory team, including resource management and team development (15 FTE) and act as the point of contact for HV testing at Zürich-Altstetten MICAFIL Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards (IEC\/IEEE) Analyze & implement efficient production & business processes to increase safety, quality and productivity  Ensure high output through reliable high-voltage testing procedures (AC, DC, impulse, partial discharge) Identify and integrate new technologies and best practices to drive the continuous improvement process (CIP) together with your team and our Process Engineering team Collaborate closely with planning, quality assurance, logistics, other production departments and relevant stakeholders for holistic process design and improvement  Be responsible for optimal work and maintenance instructions and other relevant regulations (e.g. ISO, Lean, Six Sigma) Develop and monitor the most important key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure success and re-port to operations manager As People Manager, you will be responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures and guidelines Your background Technical apprenticeship and qualifications (Bachelor \/ Master) in the field of Electrical Engineering or equivalent Several years of experience in a similar leadership position (High-Voltage Testing\/Insulation Technology\/Power Engineering) Solid knowledge in high-voltage systems, generators, and automation systems Solid knowledge of lean management, Six Sigma and other process optimization methods Proven experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams Strong problem-solving skills and a data-driven approach to decision making combined with a strong focus on safety Excellent time management, communication and organizational skills Fluent in German and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

(KPIs) to measure success and re-port to operations manager As People Manager, you will be responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures and guidelines Your background Technical apprenticeship and qualifications (Bachelor \/ Master) in the field of Electrical Engineering or equivalent Several years of experience in a similar leadership position (High-Voltage Testing\/Insulation Technology\/Power Engineering) Solid knowledge in high-voltage systems, generators, and automation systems Solid knowledge of lean management, Six Sigma and other process optimization methods Proven experience in leading and motivating cross-functional teams Strong problem-solving skills and a data-driven approach to decision making combined with a strong focus on safety Excellent time management, communication and organizational skills Fluent in German and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.
descriptionByLocale.en: The opportunity Our local product group Insulation & Components is the technology leader in design and manufacture of high volt-age transformer bushings. All renowned international companies of the electrical industry, active in power genera-tion, transmission and distribution are among our customers. We have a top position in the high voltage trans-former bushing areas worldwide. In this highly responsible and dynamic role, you will lead our laboratory team at MICAFIL in Zürich-Altstetten (ZH) and ensure that we further strengthen our competitiveness and develop our Swiss branch to meet the global market growth in the field of energy. In this important role, you will continuously drive forward the efficient and innovative processes of our 3 different laboratories together with a strong team. If you have a passion for leadership and high voltage testing, we offer you the stage to bring about real change and actively shape the future of our production processes. How you'll make an impact Lead the laboratory team, including resource management and team development (15 FTE) and act as the point of contact for HV testing at Zürich-Altstetten MICAFIL Ensure compliance with safety and quality standards (IEC\/IEEE) Analyze & implement efficient production & business processes to increase safety, quality and productivity  Ensure high output through reliable high-voltage testing procedures (AC, DC, impulse, partial discharge) Identify and integrate new technologies and best practices to drive the continuous improvement process (CIP) together with your jobs.title: Praktikant Praxisintegriertes Bachelorstudium (PiBs) Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen 40% (m/w/d)
jobs.description:
Über die Position Starte deine Karriere mit einem praxisnahen Einstieg in die Industrie und gestalte unsere Produktion durch datengetriebene Prozessoptimierung aktiv mit. Du setzt dein theoretisches Wissen aus dem Studium – etwa aus Methoden- und Projektmanagement – direkt in die Praxis um und sammelst dabei wertvolle Erfahrungen. 
• Wir bieten dir ein Umfeld, das dir Sicherheit und Selbstvertrauen für deinen Berufseinstieg gibt. Flexible Arbeitsbedingungen und ein engagiertes, unterstützendes Team begleiten dich auf deinem Weg zum erfolgreichen Bachelorabschluss. Hinweis: Das Praktikum ist ausschliesslich in Kombination mit einem praxisintegrierten Bachelorstudium an der Fachhochschule Nordwestschweiz FHNW \/ Zürcher Hochschule für angewandte Wissenschaften ZHAW möglich. Weitere Informationen findest du hier: Link zu FHNW: https:\/\/www.fhnw.ch\/de\/studium\/technik\/pibs Link zu ZHAW: https:\/\/www.zhaw.ch\/de\/engineering\/studium\/bachelorstudium\/praxisintegriertes-bachelorstudium\/ Das kannst du bewirken Automatisiere Geschäftsprozesse mit Power BI, Power Automate oder Power Platform Betreue und begleite sowohl bestehende als auch neue Projekte Arbeite mit Manufacturing Equipment Systems (MES) und Datenbanken Erstelle Prozessabläufe nach BPMN 2.0 und treibe die Digitalisierung (Process Mining, Citizen Development und Businessanalysen) voran Verbinde technisches Know-how mit betriebswirtschaftlichem Denken und setze die Theorie in Praxis um (Requirement Engineering) Lerne bei uns wie man verschiedene Anspruchsgruppen abholt um erfolgreich Wirkung zu erzielen (Change Management) Das bringst du mit Abgeschlossene Gymnasiale Matura (Voraussetzungen für die Zulassung an der Fachhochschule FHNW\/ZHAW) Offenheit, Proaktivität und Selbstmotivation Ausgeprägte Kommunikationsfähigkeit und soziale Kompetenz Fähigkeit zur eigenständigen und strukturierten Arbeitsweise Fliessendes Deutsch (mindestens C1), Kenntnisse in Englisch (B1) Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitspraktiken helfen dir, persönliche und berufliche Ziele zu verbinden. 
• Wir bieten dir ein Umfeld, in dem du deine Fähigkeiten entwickeln und wachsen kannst – unterstützt durch Mentoring und eine inklusive Kultur. Haben wir dein Interesse Geweckt? Dann freuen wir uns auf deine Bewerbung inklusive CV, Motivationsschreiben und Zeugnisse.

jobs.title: Field Service Engineer (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
Die Möglichkeit Als Field Service Engineer bei HITACHI ENERGY LTD bist du für die Installation, Wartung und Reparatur unserer Produkte und Systeme direkt beim Kunden in der Schweiz verantwortlich. Du stellst die optimale Leistung und Verfügbarkeit der Anlagen sicher und leistest damit einen wesentlichen Beitrag zur Zufriedenheit unserer Kunden. Das kannst du bewirken Durchführung von Installationen, Inbetriebnahmen, Wartungen und Reparaturen an HITACHI ENERGY Produkten und Systemen, insbesondere in den Bereichen Hochspannung, Energieübertragung und -verteilung. Fehleranalyse und -behebung an komplexen elektrotechnischen Anlagen und Systemen. Erstellung von Serviceberichten und Dokumentationen nach jedem Einsatz. Beratung der Kunden bezüglich technischer Fragestellungen und Optimierungsmöglichkeiten. Schulung von Kundenpersonal im Umgang mit den Anlagen. Einhaltung von Sicherheitsvorschriften und Qualitätsstandards. Bereitschaft zu Reisetätigkeiten innerhalb der Schweiz und gelegentlich ins Ausland. Teilnahme am Bereitschaftsdienst (Pikett) ausserhalb der regulären Arbeitszeiten. Kontinuierliche Weiterbildung und Aktualisierung des Fachwissens über neue Produkte und Technologien. Dein Hintergrund Abgeschlossene technische Ausbildung (z.B. Elektriker\/in, Elektroniker\/in, Automatiker\/in) oder Studium (z.B. Elektrotechnik, Energietechnik). Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Bereich Service oder Wartung von elektrischen Anlagen, idealerweise in der Energiebranche. Fundierte Kenntnisse in Hochspannungstechnik, Schaltanlagen und Steuerungssystemen sind von Vorteil. Sicherer Umgang mit Mess- und Prüfgeräten. Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (Schweizerdeutsch von Vorteil), sowie gute Englischkenntnisse. Französischkenntnisse sind ein Plus. Ausgeprägte Problemlösungsfähigkeiten und analytisches Denkvermögen. Hohes Mass an Eigeninitiative, Verantwortungsbewusstsein und Kundenorientierung. Gültiger Führerausweis Kategorie B. Bereitschaft zu flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und Reisetätigkeit. Mehr über uns 
• Wir bieten spannende und abwechslungsreiche 
• Aufgaben mit großem Gestaltungsspielraum sowie attraktive Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten. Dich erwarten interessante und vielseitige Tätigkeiten und Projekte, einschließlich Offshore‑Einsätzen, ein eingespieltes und dynamisches Team und eine offene Unternehmenskultur. Darüber hinaus profitierst du von einem attraktiven Leistungspaket mit festen Reise‑ und Verpflegungspauschalen sowie einem Mitspracherecht bei der Wahl der Unterkunft.

Dein Hintergrund Abgeschlossene technische Ausbildung (z.B. Elektriker\/in, Elektroniker\/in, Automatiker\/in) oder Studium (z.B. Elektrotechnik, Energietechnik). Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Bereich Service oder Wartung von elektrischen Anlagen, idealerweise in der Energiebranche. Fundierte Kenntnisse in Hochspannungstechnik, Schaltanlagen und Steuerungssystemen sind von Vorteil. Sicherer Umgang mit Mess- und Prüfgeräten. Gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (Schweizerdeutsch von Vorteil), sowie gute Englischkenntnisse. Französischkenntnisse sind ein Plus. Ausgeprägte Problemlösungsfähigkeiten und analytisches Denkvermögen. Hohes Mass an Eigeninitiative, Verantwortungsbewusstsein und Kundenorientierung. Gültiger Führerausweis Kategorie B. Bereitschaft zu flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und Reisetätigkeit. Mehr über uns 
• Wir bieten spannende und abwechslungsreiche 
• Aufgaben mit großem Gestaltungsspielraum sowie attraktive Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten. Dich erwarten interessante und vielseitige Tätigkeiten und Projekte, einschließlich Offshore‑Einsätzen, ein eingespieltes und dynamisches Team und eine offene Unternehmenskultur. Darüber hinaus profitierst du von einem attraktiven Leistungspaket mit festen Reise‑ und Verpflegungspauschalen sowie einem Mitspracherecht bei der Wahl der Unterkunft.

jobs.title: Field Service Technician (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Hitachi Energy, High Voltage Products, is renowned worldwide as a specialist for power transmission and distribution. The Service unit is responsible for the worldwide after sales support of generator circuit-breaker (GCB) systems and gas insulated switchgears (GIS), including training, installation and commissioning, scheduled maintenance, added value services, troubleshooting, extensions, retrofits and replacements. Service is the key to success. As a colleague in our globally active service team, you will appreciate the independent, varied and solution-oriented work. After a versatile, practice-oriented training in Switzerland and abroad, you will lead new GIS installations around the globe. A technically challenging position in a multicultural environment awaits you. You can expect an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow in it. Senior as well as junior applications are welcome! How you will make an impact Managing multicultural construction sites worldwide for gas insulated switchgear for the voltage level of 110KV - 550KV. Responsible for the successful execution of on-site installation \/ maintenance \/ repairs in compliance with contractual deadlines and in compliance with local legal requirements. Responsible for the quality of work performed on site through quality control and assessment of skills of local support staff. Conduct on-site product training on the product and during installation (optional). Coordination of local labor. Cost control of resources used and regular reporting to project management. Actively contribute to process improvements and proactively take measures to increase the efficiency of on-site activities. Your background Completed apprenticeship in the metal construction, electrical or automotive industry, or similar Basic knowledge in electrical engineering is a desirable must, further training in electrical engineering is an advantage Work experience in assembly & commissioning is an advantage Very good German and good English skills, at least B2 Willingness for national and especially international worldwide assignments with up to 90 % travel time Flexible, resilient and communicative team player Flair for pragmatic and unconventional solutions with high quality and safety standards More about us Exciting and varied tasks with great creative freedom Attractive training and development opportunities Interesting and very varied activities\/projects, including off-shore assignments Well-established and dynamic team Open corporate culture An attractive benefits package including fixed, travel and meal expenses, as well as a say in the choice of accommodation

jobs.title: Project Manager Railways Projects 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Are you an experienced Project Manager in the industrial sector or within railway application projects? Do you combine strong organizational skills with the ability to deliver high‑quality commercial and technical solutions for customers? Then this could be the right opportunity for you. We are looking for a Project Development and Change Manager who will oversee railway projects of varying size and complexity and support the organization in delivering the right products and service solutions to our customers. How you'll make an impact Drives adherence to contractual milestones by continuously monitoring project progress, including identified risks and opportunities from project kick-off onward Oversees project execution in line with best practices, HE policies, and guidelines, while controlling progress and resource utilization Represents the customer voice internally, acting as key customer contact and escalation point, and building strong, trust-based stakeholder relationships Provides regular and structured project reporting to the PMO through periodic review meetings Facilitates effective and timely communication with internal project stakeholders and supports information flow as required Applies HE Tier accountability principles to escalate critical deviations or customer claims and supports corrective actions and escalations Manages customer change requests by collecting, documenting, and coordinating variation offers with stakeholders, and following through until order, delivery, and invoicing Monitors project financials in close collaboration with the project controller, maintains documentation accuracy, and drives the capture and sharing of lessons learned Your background Bachelor or master's degree in mechanical engineering or a related field 2–3 years of experience as a Project Manager in railway or industrial application projects PMP certification is an advantage Very good knowledge of MS Office tools (Teams, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) Ability to motivate and empower team members Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly convey information, align stakeholders, and manage expectations Fluency in French, B2 level in English; German is a plus More about us   Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Field Service Technician (w/m/d)
jobs.description:
Die Möglichkeit Hitachi Energy High Voltage Products ist der weltweit anerkannte Spezialist für Energieübertragung und Energieverteilung. Die Serviceeinheit ist für den weltweiten After Sales Support von Generatorenschutzschaltersystemen (GCB) und gasisolierten Schaltanlagen (GIS) inklusive Schulungen, Installation und Inbetriebnahme, planmäßiger Wartung, Zusatzleistungen, Troubleshooting, Erweiterungen, Umbauten und Austausch zuständig. Service ist der Schlüssel zum Erfolg. Als Kollege\/-in in unserem weltweit tätigen Service Team schätzen Sie die selbstständige, abwechslungsreiche und lösungsorientierte Arbeit. Nach einer vielseitigen, praxisorientierten Ausbildung in der Schweiz und im Ausland, werden Sie rund um den Globus GIS Installationen und Wartungen leiten. Es erwartet Sie eine technisch herausfordernde Stelle in einem multikulturellen Umfeld. Das können Sie bewirken Leitung von multikulturellen Baustellen weltweit für gasisolierte Schaltanlagen für das Spannungslevel von 110KV – 550KV Verantwortlich für die erfolgreiche Durchführung der vor Ort Installation, Wartungen und Reparaturen unter Einhaltung vertraglicher Termine und unter Berücksichtigung lokaler gesetzlicher Vorgaben Verantwortlich für die Qualität der vor Ort ausgeführten Arbeiten mittels Qualitätskontrolle und Beurteilung der Fähigkeiten von lokalen Hilfskräften Durchführen von Produkttraining vor Ort am Produkt und während der Installation Verantwortlich für die Evaluierung von Risiken und deren Minimierung. Koordination und Anweisung von Arbeitskräften Kostenkontrolle der eingesetzten Ressourcen und regelmäßiges Rapportieren an die Projektleitung Aktives Mitarbeiten an Prozessverbesserungen und proaktives Ergreifen von Maßnahmen zur Effizienzsteigerung der Tätigkeiten vor Ort Ihr Hintergrund Abgeschlossene Lehre in der Metallbau-, Elektro- oder Automobilbranche Elektrotechnische Grundkenntnisse sind ein Muss, elektrotechnische Weiterbildung von Vorteil Mindestens 5 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Montage- und Inbetriebsetzungsbereich Sehr gute Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse, mindestens B2 Bereitschaft zu nationalen und vor allem internationalen weltweiten Einsätzen mit bis zu 80 % Reisetätigkeit Flexibler, belastbarer und kommunikativer Teamplayer Flair für pragmatische und unkonventionelle Lösungssuche mit hohen Qualitäts- und Sicherheitsstandards Mehr über uns Spannende und abwechslungsreiche 
• Aufgaben mit viel Gestaltungsspielraum Attraktive Möglichkeiten zur Weiterbildung und persönlichen Entwicklung Interessante und vielfältige Tätigkeiten\/Projekte – auch mit Auslandseinsätzen Eingespieltes und dynamisches Team Offene Unternehmenskultur Attraktives Leistungspaket inklusive fixer Vergütung, Reise- und Verpflegungskosten sowie Mitspracherecht bei der Wahl der Unterkunft

jobs.title: Teamleader Design Engineering - Generator Circuit Breaker 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Join our Generator Circuit Breaker (GCB) team and contribute to products that shape the reliability and performance of power generation worldwide. In this role, you will lead and support a motivated team of CAD Designers while collaborating closely with experienced engineers, project leads suppliers, and the in Oerlikon located factory across the entire product lifecycle—from early design concepts to continuous improvement initiatives. You will have the chance to make a real impact by driving high quality design practices, fostering unified working methods, and championing process and documentation excellence. Through a structured introduction program, you will gain the independence and confidence needed to steer your team effectively, while benefiting from targeted training and development opportunities that support your professional growth. How you'll make an impact Coach and lead a team of CAD Designers, ensuring high quality creation, modification, and maintenance of engineering data for generator circuit-breaker systems and components (team size approx. 10 FTE) Develop\/enhance\/maintain master data of all GCB products with 3D-models, drawings and bill of materials in CAD\/PLM systems  Implement special designs, cost-saving projects and drive product quality improvements Initiate and establish design-process improvements Ensure efficient collaboration with other engineering functions, project leads and further stakeholders Contribute to apprentice supervision and training together with the apprentice advisors of the team As People Manager, you will be responsible to ensure compliance with applicable external and internal regulations, procedures and guidelines Your background Solid technical foundation as senior designer or equivalent engineering background Proven experience and confident handling of CAD tools (preferably Creo) Strong working knowledge of PLM systems (preferably PTC Windchill), roles, object management, and release processes Experience in a team leading function or as a project manager of a project team Distinct self-organization skills, as well as precise, independent working style Ability to structure work, prioritize tasks, and manage multiple design topics simultaneously with high quality and reliability Mindset oriented toward process improvement, document quality, and establishing structured working methods Fluent in German and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Umweltspezialist 80 - 100% (w/m/d)
jobs.description: Über die Position Gestalte aktiv die Zukunft mit – dort, wo Sicherheit und Innovation aufeinandertreffen. Wir suchen eine engagierte und fachkompetente Umweltspezialistin bzw. einen engagierten und fachkompetenten Umweltspezialisten, die\/der unser Bestreben nach einem sicheren, gesunden und nachhaltigen Arbeitsumfeld weiter stärkt. In dieser zentralen Funktion verantwortest du Initiativen zum Schutz unserer Mitarbeitenden, stellst die Einhaltung relevanter Vorschriften sicher und förderst eine proaktive Sicherheits- und Umweltkultur im gesamten Unternehmen. Das kannst du bewirken Sicherstellen der Einhaltung von Umwelt‑ und Sicherheitsvorschriften sowie interner Richtlinien Durchführen von Risiko‑, Gefahren‑ und Umweltanalysen inklusive Ableitung von Massnahmen Planen und Durchführen von internen Audits, Begehungen und Umweltkontrollen Untersuchen von Unfällen und Ereignissen sowie Erarbeitung von Präventionsmassnahmen Mitarbeit an Nachhaltigkeit‑ und Umweltprojekten sowie Förderung der Umweltkultur Erstellen und Durchführen von Schulungen zu Umwelt‑ und Sicherheitsrelevanten Themen Beratung und Unterstützung von Führungskräften und Projektteams bei HSE‑Fragestellungen Zusammenarbeit mit Behörden, Auditoren und externen Partnern Das bringst du mit Abschluss in Umweltwissenschaften, Umwelttechnik, Arbeitssicherheit oder einem vergleichbaren technischen\/naturwissenschaftlichen Bereich (FH\/Universität) Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im HSE oder Umweltbereich, idealerweise in Industrie, Produktion oder technischen Organisationen Fundierte Kenntnisse der Schweizer Arbeitssicherheits- und Umweltgesetzgebung, inklusive relevanter Verordnungen (z. B. Störfallverordnung, Chemikalienrecht, Umweltschutzrecht) Erfahrung in der Durchführung von Risikoanalysen, Gefahrenermittlungen sowie Unfall und Ereignisabklärungen, inklusive Ableitung von Massnahmen Praxis in der Planung, Durchführung und Nachbereitung von Audits, Inspektionen und internen Begehungen, idealerweise gemäss ISO 14001 \/ ISO 45001 Starke Kommunikations- und Beratungskompetenzen für die Unterstützung von Führungskräften, Projektleitenden und Mitarbeitenden in HSE-Belangen Fähigkeit zur Erstellung und Durchführung von Schulungen zu Arbeitssicherheit, Umwelt und Nachhaltigkeit, sowie Analytische Fähigkeiten und Erfahrung im Umgang mit HSE-Kennzahlen, Reporting und kontinuierlicher Verbesserung Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie gute Englischkenntnisse, weitere Landessprachen von Vorteil Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmodelle helfen Ihnen, persönliche und berufliche Leistung zu optimieren und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeitenden ihre Fähigkeiten weiterentwickeln und wachsen können.

Das kannst du bewirken Sicherstellen der Einhaltung von Umwelt‑ und Sicherheitsvorschriften sowie interner Richtlinien Durchführen von Risiko‑, Gefahren‑ und Umweltanalysen inklusive Ableitung von Massnahmen Planen und Durchführen von internen Audits, Begehungen und Umweltkontrollen Untersuchen von Unfällen und Ereignissen sowie Erarbeitung von Präventionsmassnahmen Mitarbeit an Nachhaltigkeit‑ und Umweltprojekten sowie Förderung der Umweltkultur Erstellen und Durchführen von Schulungen zu Umwelt‑ und Sicherheitsrelevanten Themen Beratung und Unterstützung von Führungskräften und Projektteams bei HSE‑Fragestellungen Zusammenarbeit mit Behörden, Auditoren und externen Partnern Das bringst du mit Abschluss in Umweltwissenschaften, Umwelttechnik, Arbeitssicherheit oder einem vergleichbaren technischen\/naturwissenschaftlichen Bereich (FH\/Universität) Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im HSE oder Umweltbereich, idealerweise in Industrie, Produktion oder technischen Organisationen Fundierte Kenntnisse der Schweizer Arbeitssicherheits- und Umweltgesetzgebung, inklusive relevanter Verordnungen (z. B. Störfallverordnung, Chemikalienrecht, Umweltschutzrecht) Erfahrung in der Durchführung von Risikoanalysen, Gefahrenermittlungen sowie Unfall und Ereignisabklärungen, inklusive Ableitung von Massnahmen Praxis in der Planung, Durchführung und Nachbereitung von Audits, Inspektionen und internen Begehungen, idealerweise gemäss ISO 14001 \/ ISO 45001 Starke Kommunikations- und Beratungskompetenzen für die Unterstützung von Führungskräften, Projektleitenden und Mitarbeitenden in HSE-Belangen Fähigkeit zur Erstellung und Durchführung von Schulungen zu Arbeitssicherheit, Umwelt und Nachhaltigkeit, sowie Analytische Fähigkeiten und Erfahrung im Umgang mit HSE-Kennzahlen, Reporting und kontinuierlicher Verbesserung Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse sowie gute Englischkenntnisse, weitere Landessprachen von Vorteil Mehr über uns Unsere flexiblen Arbeitsmodelle helfen Ihnen, persönliche und berufliche Leistung zu optimieren und schaffen ein Umfeld, in dem alle Mitarbeitenden ihre Fähigkeiten weiterentwickeln und wachsen können.

jobs.title: Hub Europe Quality & Continuous Improvement Manager - Grid Automation
jobs.description: The Opportunity We are looking for an experienced Quality leader to drive the Quality and Continuous Improvement (CI) agenda across our Hub Europe. In this key leadership role, you will own the Quality strategy, strengthen our Quality Management Systems, ensure customer satisfaction, and lead certification, audits, and continuous improvement initiatives across all entities in the Hub. Where CI is in scope, you will also deploy a sustainable CI culture and improvement structure. The location of this role is flexible within Europe, and will be determined in later stages. How you will make an impact Quality & CI Leadership Define and implement the Hub Quality and CI strategy. Ensure alignment with global directives, regulatory requirements, and business needs. Drive capability building and governance across Hub Quality and CI teams. Customer Focus Embed the Voice of the Customer in all Quality initiatives. Represent the company in customer discussions on quality matters. Evaluate risks and implement solutions to secure high customer satisfaction. Quality Management System & Certification Lead the development and maintenance of the Quality Management System. Oversee internal\/external audits and ensure timely closure of actions. Secure required certifications across all Hub units. Performance & Improvement Monitor process and offer performance; analyze trends and drive corrective actions. Identify improvement opportunities and collaborate with stakeholders to implement changes. Escalate supplier quality issues and ensure resolution. Non‑Conformance Management Manage high‑profile NCRs and customer interventions. Ensure effective containment, corrective, and preventive measures to protect customer trust. Continuous Improvement Deploy the Continuous Improvement Framework (Lighthouse, L6S, Lessons Learned, etc.). Support and prioritize improvement projects that elevate quality and operational performance. Your background Bachelor's degree in a related field; a Master's degree or MBA is an advantage. 10–15 years of experience in a similar role within a multinational environment. Strong leadership experience in Quality Management; CI leadership is a plus. Ability to analyse performance, solve problems systematically, and manage risks. Solid influencing, communication, change management, and conflict‑resolution skills. Customer‑centric mindset and proven ability to collaborate across functions.

jobs.title: Electrical Design Engineer - Dry Transformers 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Joining our Dry Transformer engineering team, you'll help shape next generation designs that improve safety, efficiency, and sustainability in critical power applications. You'll collaborate closely with R&D, production, quality, and supply chain to translate customer requirements into manufacturable solutions and see them through prototype, type testing, and serial release. Working across global projects and standards, you'll deepen your expertise in insulation systems, electromagnetic design, and thermal performance while contributing to the digitalization of our design processes. This is a high impact role where your work directly enables reliable electricity for industries and communities worldwide. How you'll make an impact Lead the development of design specifications and models, ensuring they meet all relevant standards and regulations Conduct thorough design reviews and verifications, implementing modifications to elevate project quality Monitor and manage project timelines, ensuring design milestones are achieved and projects are delivered on schedule  Conduct risk assessments, develop mitigation strategies, and seek opportunities for design innovations Engage with customers to capture and incorporate their feedback into design solutions, enhancing customer satisfaction Collaborate with the supply chain team to ensure designs are manufacturable and cost-effective, selecting optimal materials and components Prepare and maintain detailed documentation that supports design processes and facilitates knowledge sharing Identify and implement continuous design improvements based on test results and industry best practices, enhancing quality and efficiency Your background Degree in electrical engineering or a related field 2-5 years of experience in electrical design engineering of dry type transformers Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team  English is a must, Italian is beneficial Pro-active attitude, self-motivated and with a strong sense of ownership Open to change, feedback and continuous improvement. More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Commissioning Engineer 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
The opportunity   At Hitachi Energy, our purpose is to advance a sustainable energy future for all. We bring power to homes, schools, hospitals, and industries — and we do so with people who are passionate, collaborative, and committed to making a real difference. As a SCADA\/RTU Commissioning Engineer, you will join our Grid Automation Systems team in Baden, Switzerland. In this role, you will oversee the commissioning of substation automation systems for medium‑complexity assignments, ensuring all activities are completed safely, efficiently, and in line with contract requirements, quality standards, and safety expectations. How you'll make an impact  
• Carry out testing and commissioning of substation automation systems at customer sites in Switzerland and abroad, while actively supporting the Service Management team
• Execute commissioning activities safely, following all Hitachi Energy and customer safety standards; report hazards and near misses to support continuous improvement
• Perform commissioning tasks according to defined process descriptions and checklists; coordinate closely with engineering and project management teams
• Identify, track, and resolve open points, ensuring customer sign‑off before site demobilization
• Report site progress and planning updates; ensure efficient use of commissioning tools and test equipment
• Provide marked‑up drawings to engineering for preparing as‑built documentation and final submissions
• Support planning and implementation of commissioning activities
• Implement design changes and provide mark‑ups for as‑built project documentation
• Coordinate site activities required to finalize customer acceptance certification   Your background  
• Bachelor's degree in electrical engineering or equivalent
• 4+ years of experience as a commissioning engineer for automation systems and related products
• Strong knowledge of substation SCADA\/RTU systems, HMI principles, and Hitachi Energy products; understanding of protection philosophies
• Basic knowledge of communication protocols such as IEC 61850, LON, IEC 101–104, DNP; ability to read schematic drawings and identify discrepancies; strong problem‑solving and conflict‑resolution skills
• Familiarity with engineering toolchains typical for SAS\/SCADA (IET600, PCM600, MicroSCADA); willingness to learn across SCADA, RTU, networking, and cybersecurity topics
• Strong communication and presentation skills
• Proficiency in spoken and written English, German is preferred More about us At Hitachi Energy, we believe that diversity and collaboration are the foundation of great innovation. You will join a global team where different perspectives are valued, and where we work together to advance a sustainable energy future for all.

You will join a global team where different perspectives are valued, and where we work together to advance a sustainable energy future for all.
descriptionByLocale.en:
The opportunity   At Hitachi Energy, our purpose is to advance a sustainable energy future for all. We bring power to homes, schools, hospitals, and industries — and we do so with people who are passionate, collaborative, and committed to making a real difference. As a SCADA\/RTU Commissioning Engineer, you will join our Grid Automation Systems team in Baden, Switzerland. In this role, you will oversee the commissioning of substation automation systems for medium‑complexity assignments, ensuring all activities are completed safely, efficiently, and in line with contract requirements, quality standards, and safety expectations. How you'll make an impact  
• Carry out testing and commissioning of substation automation systems at customer sites in Switzerland and abroad, while actively supporting the Service Management team
• Execute commissioning activities safely, following all Hitachi Energy and customer safety standards; report hazards and near misses to support continuous improvement
• Perform commissioning tasks according to defined process descriptions and checklists; coordinate closely with engineering and project management teams
• Identify, track, and resolve open points, ensuring customer sign‑off before site demobilization
• Report site progress and planning updates; ensure efficient use of commissioning tools and test equipment
• Provide marked‑up drawings to engineering for preparing as‑built documentation and final submissions
• Support planning and implementation of commissioning activities
• Implement design changes and provide mark‑ups for as‑built project documentation
• Coordinate site activities required to finalize customer acceptance certification   Your background  
• Bachelor's degree in electrical engineering or equivalent
• 4+ years of experience as a commissioning engineer for automation systems and related products
• Strong knowledge of substation SCADA\/RTU systems, HMI principles, and Hitachi Energy products; understanding of protection philosophies
• Basic knowledge of communication protocols such as IEC 61850, LON, IEC 101–104, DNP; ability to read schematic drawings and identify discrepancies; strong problem‑solving and conflict‑resolution skills
• Familiarity with engineering toolchains typical for SAS\/SCADA (IET600, PCM600, MicroSCADA); willingness to learn across SCADA, RTU, networking, and cybersecurity topics
• Strong communication and presentation skills
• Proficiency in spoken and written English, German is preferred More about us At Hitachi Energy, we believe that diversity and collaboration are the foundation of great innovation. You will join a global team where different perspectives are valued, and where we work together to advance a sustainable energy future for all.

jobs.title: On-Site IT Service Specialist 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity In your role as an On-site IT Service Specialist, you will   be responsible for   delivering Desk Side Support to IT users, specifically handling IT issues that require physical intervention. Your duties will extend to aiding incident resolution through collaboration with various support teams and groups within the IT department. You will play a critical role, serving as a representative of the IT organization, acting as the interface between the customer and other IT functions.   How you'll make an impact Diagnoses and resolves IT-related issues effectively and efficiently Ensures that computers are delivered in a 'ready-to-use' condition for IT users Provides ad-hoc training to users on common IT issues and guides them through self-service tools Handles warranty cases in collaboration with the appropriate Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEM) vendors Identifies and report potential risks that could impact the quality of OSS services Offers support to end users and ensure the functionality of IT equipment and software in meeting rooms and other user collaboration areas as part of desk-side support Adheres to prescribed OSS governance, which involves participating in meetings and following the escalation process, among other responsibilities Follows OSS procedures as outlined in the Runbook, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and knowledge articles   Your background Formal qualification in IT completed from an accredited university or college OR completed an apprenticeship in the field of   IT ​ Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, including the ability to communicate effectively with both business   and technical audiences in German and English. ​ Proactive and solution-oriented mindset   ​ Demonstrates an ability to work independently and is self-driven ​ Legal authorization to work in applied location is   required . We will not sponsor individuals for employment visas now or in   the future for this role ​ More about us Hitachi Energy is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive, and collaborative work environment where people from different backgrounds come together to build innovative solutions for a more sustainable energy future.

jobs.title: Service Project Manager Transformers 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity  Join our Local Service Center Switzerland for a dynamic position that blends project management with hands-on field responsibilities. In this role, you will oversee power transformer service projects from start to finish, ensuring all tools, equipment, and calibration procedures are meticulously prepared for safe and efficient onsite operations. By joining our team, you will contribute directly to the stability of Switzerland's electrical grid and have the opportunity to collaborate with key players in the Swiss energy market.   If you enjoy managing stakeholders as much as rolling up your sleeves to make sure field teams are ready for action, this is the role for you.  Please submit your documents in English.   How You'll Make an Impact   Lead and coordinate cross‑functional transformer service projects end‑to‑end, onsite execution, and close‑out, with full ownership of schedule, cost, quality, resources, and risks  Ensure availability, compliance, and reliability of tools, instruments, and testing equipment, including maintenance, calibration, certification, and readiness for transformer service operations  Manage and optimize tooling and equipment logistics, covering inventory control, storage, replenishment, shipment, project‑specific material preparation, and resource sharing across projects  Support field teams with complete job packs, including technical documentation, procedures, safety requirements, and onsite coordination when required  Act as the primary interface between customers, field service teams, workshops, suppliers, and internal stakeholders, ensuring clear, proactive, and satisfaction‑oriented communication throughout the project lifecycle  Plan, schedule, and supervise service activities, ensuring full job readiness prior to site mobilization, including technical scope, documentation, permits, access, logistics, and HSE prerequisites  Prepare and coordinate onsite activities, guarantee safe, smooth, and efficient execution, and providing hands‑on operational support during critical or complex situations  Identify, escalate, and resolve project and operational risks, ensuring strict compliance with HSE rules, ISO standards, and regulatory requirements  Drive continuous improvement and standardization, enhancing tooling management, site preparation processes, and overall service execution efficiency  Your background  Degree in engineering, technical management, or a related field   3+ years of project management or field service coordination experience   Handson mindset and strong interest in tools, calibration, safety, and technical preparation   Solid organizational and communication skills   Experience with quality standards (e.g., ISO)   Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks end-to-end   Business fluent in French and fluent English; German is a plus   More about us   Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

jobs.title: Field Service Engineer​ – Energy & Power (f/m/d)
jobs.description: The opportunity Our High Voltage Products division is a worldwide specialist in power transmission and distribution. Our Service Unit supports customers around the globe with Generator Circuit Breaker systems (GCB) and gas‑insulated switchgear (GIS). From training and commissioning to maintenance, troubleshooting, upgrades, and replacements — we ensure critical power infrastructure works reliably, safely, and efficiently. Service is at the heart of everything we do. As part of our international service team, you'll take on varied, hands‑on work and become a recognized product expert. After comprehensive, practical training in Switzerland, you'll travel the world to commission and maintain GCB systems — all while working in a dynamic, multicultural environment. This is a role with real impact, technical depth, and plenty of room to grow. How you´ll make an impact Lead multicultural service projects for GCB on power plants from 20 MW to 1600 MW worldwide Ensure smooth and timely execution of installation and maintenance work on site Perform the final quality check and guarantee that all work meets our high standards Deliver product training directly to customers at their facilities Identify risks proactively and implement effective mitigation measures Coordinate on‑site teams and give practical, clear instructions Manage resource and provide regular reporting to project management Drive process improvements and implement efficiency‑boosting initiatives on site Your background Completed apprenticeship in metalwork, electrical engineering, or automotive technology Solid electrical knowledge (essential); further education such as a technician diploma is a plus Minimum 2 years of professional experience; experience in site leadership is an advantage Good English skills (B2 or above); German is a plus High willingness to travel — up to 80% worldwide Flexible, resilient, and communicative team player who enjoys solving problems pragmatically and values high safety and quality standards More about us Exciting and varied tasks with great creative freedom Attractive training and development opportunities Interesting and very varied activities\/projects, including off-shore assignments Well-established and dynamic team Open corporate culture An attractive benefits package including fixed, travel and meal expenses, as well as a say in the choice of accommodation

How you´ll make an impact Lead multicultural service projects for GCB on power plants from 20 MW to 1600 MW worldwide Ensure smooth and timely execution of installation and maintenance work on site Perform the final quality check and guarantee that all work meets our high standards Deliver product training directly to customers at their facilities Identify risks proactively and implement effective mitigation measures Coordinate on‑site teams and give practical, clear instructions Manage resource and provide regular reporting to project management Drive process improvements and implement efficiency‑boosting initiatives on site Your background Completed apprenticeship in metalwork, electrical engineering, or automotive technology Solid electrical knowledge (essential); further education such as a technician diploma is a plus Minimum 2 years of professional experience; experience in site leadership is an advantage Good English skills (B2 or above); German is a plus High willingness to travel — up to 80% worldwide Flexible, resilient, and communicative team player who enjoys solving problems pragmatically and values high safety and quality standards More about us Exciting and varied tasks with great creative freedom Attractive training and development opportunities Interesting and very varied activities\/projects, including off-shore assignments Well-established and dynamic team Open corporate culture An attractive benefits package including fixed, travel and meal expenses, as well as a say in the choice of accommodation

jobs.title: Operating Unit Manager for Substation Automation Systems - Europe
jobs.description: The Opportunity As the Operating Unit Manager for Substation Automation Systems HUB Europe, you will play a pivotal leadership role in shaping the strategic direction, performance, and growth of our Substation Automation portfolio across the region. You will oversee all commercial, operational, and technical aspects of the HUB, ensuring excellence in project execution, customer satisfaction, and business results. This role reports to the HUB Leader and collaborates closely with global Product Management, R&D, and cross‑regional teams. Location is flexible within Europe and will be confirmed during later stages. This role requires frequent travel within Europe and occasional international travel. How You'll Make an Impact Drive Strategic Direction & Business Growth: Develop and execute the HUB's strategy in alignment with global objectives. Identify market opportunities, expand customer relationships, and grow sales in substation automation solutions including protection relays, SCADA, control systems, and communication technologies. Lead Operational Excellence: Oversee engineering, project delivery, sales support, and service execution. Define and track KPIs to secure efficient operations, on‑time delivery, customer satisfaction, and strong financial performance. Strengthen Customer & Market Presence: Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers, partners, and stakeholders across Europe. Represent Hitachi Energy as a subject matter expert at industry forums and customer engagements. Ensure Financial Performance: Manage budgets, forecasts, and resource allocation to achieve revenue, margin, and profitability targets. Identify improvement opportunities to enhance efficiency and reduce cost. Advance Technical Expertise & Innovation: Stay at the forefront of substation automation technologies, industry trends, competitor movements, and regulatory changes. Promote innovation and technology adoption across the HUB. Champion Quality, Safety & Compliance: Ensure operations comply with internal policies, industry standards, and European regulations. Promote a strong safety and quality culture across all teams and activities. Collaborate Across Functions: Partner with global product management, R&D, and other regional business units to share best practices, align strategies, and maximize value from the full Hitachi Energy portfolio. Lead & Develop High‑Performing Teams: Recruit, mentor, and empower a strong team of engineers, project managers, and commercial professionals. Foster a culture of collaboration, continuous learning, and operational excellence. Your Background Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering, Automation Engineering, or a related field; a Master's degree or MBA is an advantage. 10–15 years of experience in the power transmission and distribution industry, with deep expertise in substation automation systems, of which 5–7 years in a leadership role managing a business unit, department, or similar organization within a multinational environment. Strong technical knowledge across substation automation architectures, protective relaying, SCADA, control systems, IEC 61850, and cybersecurity for critical infrastructure. Proven success in business strategy execution, P&L management, contract negotiation, and complex project delivery and strong understanding of the European energy market, customer landscape, and regulatory environment. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder‑management skills with the ability to influence across a matrix environment. Fluency in English is required; additional European languages are an asset.

jobs.title: Senior R&D Electrical Engineer for Medium Frequency Transformers 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
jobs.description:
The opportunity Join our Next Generation Grids Transformers team in Geneva and shape the future of energy. As a Senior R&D Electrical Engineer, you will work on cutting-edge transformer technologies in a dynamic, international environment. You'll be part of a collaborative team, contributing to the full R&D lifecycle—from concept to testing and product launch, ensuring that our solutions meet both technical specifications and market demands. This role offers hands-on experience, technical growth, and the chance to make a real impact in the energy transition. How you will make an impact Apply your knowledge of electromagnetism to support innovative concepts' validation through simulations Tackle a variety of engineering challenges on Medium Frequency Transformers Contribute to the development of new features in our internal design tools Organize prototyping and testing activities to validate new technologies\/products in collaboration with global teams Align your work with our strategic technology roadmap Take ownership of work packages and deliver results using a structured approach Deepen your understanding of transformer technologies and support internal consulting to optimize your R&D activities Contribute to intellectual property and risk management activities in R&D Your background MSc or PhD in Electrical or Electromechanical Engineering, ideally with experience in R&D within a business environment Knowledge of electromagnetism and circuit theory with ability to perform electromagnetic simulations Proficiency in MATLAB and\/or Python Strong communication skills in English (French is a plus) Analytical thinking and ability to work independently and collaboratively within interdisciplinary and international teams Awareness of business processes related to product development More about us 
• We offer flexible working practices and a supportive environment where you can grow your skills and career. What you can expect from us Exciting career and training opportunities within Hitachi Energy Inclusive and diverse work culture Discounts on fitness memberships and various services Attractive pension benefits through our company pension fund
